assembly_instructions: Tethered DIY ROV built on two parallel laser-cut acrylic plates (top + bottom) held apart by four vertical aluminum standoffs at the corners. Between the plates: three transparent acrylic pressure vessels — a central electronics tube and two smaller battery tubes on either side — each sealed with machined endcaps. Three brushless thrusters drive the vehicle: two rear-facing horizontal thrusters (port + starboard) for surge/yaw and one vertical thruster in the centerline for heave; each has a printed propeller. A forward camera module and three LED lights ride on the front of the top plate. A neutrally-buoyant tether exits through a printed strain-relief on the top plate. A steel trim/ballast weight hangs below the bottom plate. The BeagleBone + cape controller is shown on top of the central tube for inspectability (physically it lives inside the e-tube).

info.summary: Tether-operated DIY underwater ROV from the OpenROV community. Acrylic electronics + battery tubes, three brushless thrusters, forward camera + LEDs, ~100 m depth rating.
info.description: OpenROV 2.8 is the last of the 'classic' OpenROV tethered ROVs — a citizen-science and maker-oriented underwater vehicle released as an open-hardware kit. The laser-cut acrylic twin-plate chassis carries a central watertight electronics tube (housing a BeagleBone Black + custom cape running the open OpenROV Cockpit software), two side-mounted battery tubes, and three brushless thrusters (two horizontal at the rear for surge/yaw, one vertical through the centerline for heave). A forward CMOS camera on a small tilt servo pairs with a high-output LED array for visibility in turbid water. The tether is a neutrally buoyant twisted-pair that carries Ethernet-over-powerline back to a topside router and laptop running the control UI. Nominal depth rating is around 100 m; runtime on internal C-cells is roughly 2–3 hours. The hardware and software are released under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.
info.operator: OpenROV community / Sofar Ocean builders and educators
info.status: Classic 2.x production ended; superseded by Trident. Open-hardware designs remain public.

facts.label: Chassis
facts.value: Two parallel laser-cut acrylic plates + four aluminum standoffs

facts.label: Pressure vessels
facts.value: 1 × central electronics tube + 2 × battery tubes, acrylic with machined endcaps

facts.label: Imaging
facts.value: Forward CMOS camera on a tilt servo + 3 × LED

parts.name: Top Acrylic Plate
parts.role: Laser-cut 6 mm acrylic top plate — mounts the camera, LED array, tether strain relief, and the thrusters/tubes below.

parts.name: Bottom Acrylic Plate
parts.role: Laser-cut 6 mm acrylic bottom plate — other face of the open twin-plate chassis, holds the ballast weight.

parts.name: Aluminum Standoff (Fwd-Left)
parts.role: Front-left aluminum standoff spacing the two plates.

parts.name: Aluminum Standoff (Fwd-Right)
parts.role: Front-right standoff.

parts.name: Aluminum Standoff (Rear-Left)
parts.role: Rear-left standoff.

parts.name: Aluminum Standoff (Rear-Right)
parts.role: Rear-right standoff.

parts.name: Electronics Tube (central)
parts.role: Central watertight acrylic tube housing the BeagleBone Black + OpenROV cape + tilt servo.

parts.name: E-Tube Endcap (Front)
parts.role: Machined front endcap sealing the electronics tube.

parts.name: E-Tube Endcap (Rear)
parts.role: Machined rear endcap sealing the electronics tube.

parts.name: Battery Tube (Port)
parts.role: Port-side watertight acrylic battery tube. Carries C-cells for the port power channel.

parts.name: Battery Tube (Starboard)
parts.role: Starboard-side battery tube.

parts.name: Battery Endcap (Port, Front)
parts.role: Front endcap on the port battery tube (rear endcap identical and implicit).

parts.name: Battery Endcap (Stbd, Front)
parts.role: Front endcap on the starboard battery tube.

parts.name: Thruster (Rear Port, horizontal)
parts.role: Rear horizontal brushless thruster, port side — generates surge/yaw.

parts.name: Thruster (Rear Starboard, horizontal)
parts.role: Rear horizontal brushless thruster, starboard side.

parts.name: Thruster (Center, vertical)
parts.role: Center vertical brushless thruster — generates heave (up/down).

parts.name: Propeller (Port)
parts.role: Small printed propeller on the port thruster.

parts.name: Propeller (Starboard)
parts.role: Propeller on the starboard thruster.

parts.name: Propeller (Vertical)
parts.role: Propeller on the vertical thruster.

parts.name: Forward CMOS Camera
parts.role: Forward-facing CMOS camera on a tilt servo (above the top plate, front).

parts.name: LED Light (Port)
parts.role: One of three high-output LEDs alongside the camera.

parts.name: LED Light (Center)
parts.role: Center LED.

parts.name: LED Light (Starboard)
parts.role: Starboard LED.

parts.name: Tether Strain Relief
parts.role: Printed strain-relief boot where the tether clamps to the top plate.

parts.name: Tether Cable
parts.role: Neutrally-buoyant twisted-pair tether. Carries Ethernet-over-powerline to a topside router and laptop.

parts.name: BeagleBone + Cape (shown)
parts.role: BeagleBone Black + custom OpenROV cape running the Cockpit stack. Physically lives inside the e-tube; rendered on top for inspectability.

parts.name: Trim Ballast Weight
parts.role: Small steel bar under the bottom plate, trimmed to set neutral buoyancy.
